_comment: MARCUS ECG Q&A generation templates. Used by scripts/build_dataset.py.
generation_guidance: ECG images are 12-lead waveforms rendered as a standard 4×3 grid PNG (leads I, II, III, aVR, aVL, aVF, V1–V6). Questions should require visual interpretation of waveform morphology, not just text in the report. Focus on: rhythm identification (sinus, AF, flutter, VT, SVT), conduction abnormalities (AV blocks, bundle branch blocks), ST/T-wave changes (elevation, depression, T-wave inversions, hyperacute T-waves), voltage criteria for hypertrophy, axis deviation, QRS/QT interval measurements, and specific ECG patterns (Brugada, WPW, long QT, Wellens, de Winter).

example_questions.question: What is the cardiac rhythm shown in this ECG?
Which conduction abnormality is present on this ECG?
Which leads demonstrate ST-segment elevation in this ECG?
What is the most likely diagnosis suggested by this ECG?

pretrain_focus: Report-style descriptions of ECG findings — rhythm, rate, intervals (PR, QRS, QTc), axis, and specific abnormalities found on the tracing.
sft_focus: Diverse clinical Q&A covering all ECG categories. Questions should be answerable from the image alone.
grpo_focus: Unambiguous MCQ questions with exactly one clearly correct answer and three plausible distractors. Avoid trick questions or questions requiring information not visible on the ECG.
